example_sentence: El laberinto de la soledad es una obra clave para entender la identidad mexicana.
example_translation: The Labyrinth of Solitude is a key work for understanding Mexican identity.

translation: the mask (Paz's concept of the social mask Mexicans wear)
example_sentence: Según Paz, la máscara protege al mexicano de la vulnerabilidad.
example_translation: According to Paz, the mask protects the Mexican from vulnerability.

example_sentence: La trama de la novela es compleja pero fascinante.
example_translation: The plot of the novel is complex but fascinating.

example_sentence: El desenlace de la obra sorprendió a todos.
example_translation: The ending of the work surprised everyone.

translation: to move emotionally / to touch deeply
example_sentence: La poesía de Sor Juana me conmueve profundamente.
example_translation: Sor Juana's poetry moves me deeply.

translation: to evoke / to call to mind
example_sentence: El poema evoca recuerdos de la infancia.
example_translation: The poem evokes memories of childhood.

example_sentence: El poema tiene cuatro estrofas de cuatro versos cada una.
example_translation: The poem has four stanzas of four lines each.

example_sentence: El simbolismo del agua en la novela es muy rico.
example_translation: The symbolism of water in the novel is very rich.

translation: the work is about / deals with
example_sentence: La obra trata de la búsqueda de identidad en el México moderno.
example_translation: The work deals with the search for identity in modern Mexico.

example_sentence: El trasfondo histórico de la novela es la Revolución Mexicana.
example_translation: The historical backdrop of the novel is the Mexican Revolution.

translation: it left me thinking / it gave me a lot to think about
example_sentence: Pedro Páramo me dejó pensando durante semanas.
example_translation: Pedro Páramo left me thinking for weeks.

translation: free verse (poetry without fixed rhyme or meter)
example_sentence: Mucha poesía mexicana moderna está escrita en verso libre.
example_translation: Much modern Mexican poetry is written in free verse.
